If you are using the Service on behalf of an organisation, you represent and warrant that you have authority to bind that organisation to these Terms, and references to "you" include that organisation.

---

## 1. The Service

### 1.1 Description

Lightfast is an operating infrastructure platform that connects to your software development tools, ingests engineering events, and provides semantic search, knowledge graph, and AI-powered reasoning across your team's activity. The Service enables you to observe, reason about, and act on information from your connected tools through a single interface.

### 1.2 Accounts

You must create an account to use the Service. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activity under your account. You must promptly notify us of any unauthorised use.

### 1.3 Organisations

The Service is organised around organisations. When you create or join an organisation, you agree to the access controls and permissions set by the organisation's administrators. Organisation administrators may control access to data, manage team membership, and configure integrations.

### 1.4 API Keys

You may generate API keys to access the Service programmatically. API keys are organisation-scoped credentials. You are responsible for keeping your API keys secure and must not share them publicly. We may revoke API keys that we reasonably believe have been compromised.

---

## 2. Your Data

### 2.1 Ownership

You retain all rights, title, and interest in your data. "**Your Data**" means any data, content, or information that you or your connected tools transmit to the Service, including events, entities, repository content, and configuration.

### 2.2 Licence to Lightfast

You grant Lightfast a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free licence to use, process, store, and display Your Data solely to provide, maintain, and improve the Service. This licence terminates when you delete Your Data or close your account.

### 2.3 Connected Tools

When you connect third-party tools (such as GitHub, Vercel, Linear, or Sentry) to Lightfast, you authorise us to receive data from those tools via webhooks and APIs within the scopes you approve. You are responsible for ensuring you have the right to share that data with us.

### 2.4 AI Processing

The Service uses artificial intelligence to process Your Data, including generating embeddings, extracting entities, and producing AI-generated summaries and insights. AI-generated outputs are provided for informational purposes and may not be accurate. You are responsible for verifying AI outputs before acting on them.

We do not use Your Data to train general-purpose AI models. Your Data is processed solely to provide the Service to you and your organisation.

### 2.5 Data Isolation

Your Data is isolated to your organisation. Other organisations cannot access Your Data. We implement technical controls to enforce this isolation.

---

## 3. Acceptable Use

You agree not to:

- Use the Service for any unlawful purpose or in violation of any applicable law or regulation.
- Attempt to gain unauthorised access to the Service, other accounts, or systems connected to the Service.

- Reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any part of the Service, except to the extent permitted by law.
- Use the Service to transmit malware, spam, or other harmful content.

- Use the Service in a manner that could damage, disable, or impair the Service or interfere with other users.

We may suspend or terminate your access if we reasonably believe you have violated these Terms.

---

### 4.2 Billing

We may change our pricing with at least 30 days' notice. Price changes will apply from the start of your next billing cycle after the notice period.

# Privacy Policy

**Lightfast Pty Ltd** ABN 35 690 889 984 ("**Lightfast**", "**we**", "**us**", "**our**") operates the Lightfast platform at [lightfast.ai](https://lightfast.ai).

This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information in accordance with the _Privacy Act 1988_ (Cth) and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs). It applies to all users of the Lightfast platform, website, and related services (the "**Service**").

By using the Service, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

---

## 1. Information We Collect

### 1.1 Account Information

When you create an account, we collect personal information through our authentication provider, Clerk, including:

- Email address
- Full name
- Profile photograph (if provided)
- Password or single sign-on (SSO) credentials

This information is managed by Clerk and is not stored in Lightfast's own databases. We reference your account via unique identifiers only.

### 1.2 Organisation Information

When you create or join an organisation on Lightfast, we collect:

- Organisation name and identifier
- Team membership and roles
- Billing and subscription information (processed by Clerk)

### 1.3 Connected Tool Data

When you connect third-party tools (such as GitHub, Vercel, Linear, or Sentry) to Lightfast, we collect and process:

- **OAuth tokens**: Access and refresh tokens required to communicate with your connected tools. These tokens are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM.
- **Webhook events**: Events sent from your connected tools, including pull requests, deployments, issues, errors, and other engineering activity.
- **Extracted entities**: Structured data derived from events, such as engineer names, project identifiers, endpoints, and their relationships.
- **Repository content**: If you enable repository indexing (e.g., a `.lightfast` repository), we cache specific file content to provide AI-powered context.

### 1.4 Usage and Analytics Data

We automatically collect:

- **Page views and navigation data** via PostHog, including URLs visited and anonymous usage identifiers.
- **Error and performance data** via Sentry, including error traces, browser information, and session replays (with all text masked and media blocked).
- **User agent strings** and request metadata recorded in activity audit logs.
- **IP addresses** recorded when API keys are used.

### 1.5 Information You Provide

- Feedback submitted through our in-app feedback tool (which may include screenshots).
- Communications you send to us, such as support requests or emails.

---

## 2. How We Use Your Information

We use the information we collect to:

- **Provide the Service**: Ingest events from your connected tools, build a knowledge graph of your engineering activity, and enable semantic search and AI-powered reasoning.
- **Authenticate and authorise**: Verify your identity and manage access to your organisation's data.
- **Maintain and improve**: Monitor performance, diagnose errors, and improve the Service.
- **Communicate**: Send transactional emails (e.g., invitations, notifications) via our email provider.
- **Analyse usage**: Understand how the Service is used to inform product decisions.
- **Ensure security**: Detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security incidents. Maintain audit logs of user activities.
- **Comply with law**: Meet our legal obligations, including under the _Privacy Act 1988_ (Cth).

---

## 3. How We Share Your Information

We do not sell your personal information. We share information only in the following circumstances:

### 3.1 Sub-Processors

We use third-party service providers ("sub-processors") to operate the Service. These providers process data on our behalf and are contractually required to protect your information:

| Provider | Purpose | Data Processed | Location |
|---|---|---|---|
| Clerk | Authentication and user management | Account details, sessions, billing | United States |
| Sentry | Error tracking and session replay | Error data, masked session replays | United States |
| PostHog | Product analytics | Page views, anonymous usage data | United States / EU |
| Pinecone | Vector search and embeddings | Event content embeddings | United States |
| Upstash | Caching and real-time data | Cached organisation data | United States / EU |
| Resend | Transactional email | Email addresses, email content | United States |
| Vercel | Hosting and deployment | Server logs, request data | United States |
| OpenAI | AI model processing | Event content for AI analysis | United States |
| Inngest | Background job orchestration | Event payloads, workflow state | United States |

### 3.2 Connected Tool Providers

When you connect tools to Lightfast, we exchange data with those providers (GitHub, Vercel, Linear, Sentry, Apollo) as necessary to provide the Service. The data shared is governed by each provider's own privacy policy and the OAuth scopes you authorise.

### 3.3 Legal Requirements

We may disclose information if required by law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request, or to protect the rights, property, or safety of Lightfast, our users, or the public.

### 3.4 Business Transfers

In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, your information may be transferred as part of that transaction. We will notify you of any such change.

---

## 4. Cross-Border Data Transfers

Lightfast is an Australian company, but our sub-processors are primarily located in the United States and the European Union. In accordance with APP 8, before disclosing personal information to an overseas recipient, we take reasonable steps to ensure that the recipient handles your information consistently with the APPs. This includes entering into contractual arrangements that require equivalent protections.

---

## 5. Data Security

We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your information, including:

- **Encryption at rest**: OAuth tokens are encrypted using AES-256-GCM. API keys are stored as SHA-256 hashes.
- **Encryption in transit**: All data transmitted between your browser and our servers uses TLS encryption.
- **Access controls**: Organisation-scoped data isolation ensures that your data is accessible only to authorised members of your organisation.
- **Session replay masking**: Sentry session replays mask all text and block all media to minimise data exposure.
- **Token scrubbing**: Authentication tokens are redacted from diagnostic breadcrumbs and logs.
- **Audit logging**: User activities are logged for security monitoring and compliance.

No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. If we become aware of a data breach that is likely to result in serious harm, we will notify affected individuals and the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) in accordance with the Notifiable Data Breaches scheme.

---

## 6. Data Retention

We retain your information for as long as your account is active or as needed to provide the Service. Specifically:

- **Account data**: Retained while your account exists. Managed by Clerk and deleted upon account closure.
- **Organisation data**: Retained while the organisation is active. Events, entities, and related data are retained for the lifetime of the organisation.
- **OAuth tokens**: Retained while the associated tool connection is active. Deleted when a connection is revoked (cascade delete).
- **Audit logs**: Retained for compliance and security purposes.
- **Analytics data**: Retained in accordance with our analytics providers' retention policies.

You may request deletion of your personal information at any time (see Section 8).

---

## 7. Cookies and Tracking Technologies

We use the following tracking technologies:

- **Essential cookies**: Set by Clerk for authentication and session management. These are necessary for the Service to function.
- **Analytics cookies**: Set by PostHog for product analytics. These help us understand how the Service is used.
- **Error tracking**: Sentry uses cookies and local storage for error reporting and session replay.

---

## 8. Your Rights

Under the Australian Privacy Principles, you have the right to:

- **Access**: Request access to the personal information we hold about you.
- **Correction**: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal information.
- **Complaint**: Lodge a complaint if you believe we have breached the APPs.
- **Deletion**: Request deletion of your personal information, subject to our legal obligations.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at **support@lightfast.ai**.

If you are not satisfied with our response, you may lodge a complaint with the [Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC)](https://www.oaic.gov.au/).

---

## 9. Third-Party Links

The Service may contain links to third-party websites or services that are not operated by us. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of those third parties. We encourage you to review their privacy policies.

---

## 10. Children's Privacy

The Service is not directed to individuals under the age of 16. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that we have collected information from a child, we will take steps to delete it.

---

## 11. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Your continued use of the Service after any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

---

## 12. Contact Us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:

**Lightfast Pty Ltd**
Email: [support@lightfast.ai](mailto:support@lightfast.ai)
Website: [lightfast.ai](https://lightfast.ai)

# Commit Changes

You are tasked with creating git commits for the changes made during this session.

## Process:

1. **Think about what changed:**
   - Review the conversation history and understand what was accomplished
   - Run `git status` to see current changes
   - Run `git diff` to understand the modifications
   - Consider whether changes should be one commit or multiple logical commits

2. **Plan your commit(s):**
   - Identify which files belong together
   - Draft clear, descriptive commit messages
   - Use imperative mood in commit messages
   - Focus on why the changes were made, not just what

3. **Present your plan to the user:**
   - List the files you plan to add for each commit
   - Show the commit message(s) you'll use
   - Ask: "I plan to create [N] commit(s) with these changes. Shall I proceed?"

4. **Execute upon confirmation:**
   - Use `git add` with specific files (never use `-A` or `.`)
   - Create commits with your planned messages
   - Show the result with `git log --oneline -n [number]`

## Remember:

- You have the full context of what was done in this session
- Group related changes together
- Keep commits focused and atomic when possible
- The user trusts your judgment - they asked you to commit

-# Issue Investigation
-
-Sentry issues represent grouped error events. The CLI provides four operations for working with issues.
-
-## Workflow
-
-The typical investigation flow:
-
-```
-sentry issue list          → find the issue
-sentry issue view <ID>     → see stack trace and details
-sentry issue explain <ID>  → AI root cause analysis (Seer)
-sentry issue plan <ID>     → AI solution plan (Seer)
-```
-
-## Running Commands
-
-All commands must run from an app directory with `pnpm with-env`:
-
-```bash
-cd apps/<app> && pnpm with-env npx sentry issue <subcommand>
-```
-
-The CLI auto-detects the project from `SENTRY_ORG` and `SENTRY_PROJECT` in the loaded env.
-
-## Issue IDs
-
-Issues have two ID formats:
-
-- **Short ID**: Human-readable, e.g. `LIGHTFAST-APP-4T` or `LIGHTFAST-PLATFORM-1`
-- **Numeric ID**: Internal, e.g. `6273481923`
-
-Both work with `view`, `explain`, and `plan` commands. The short ID is shown in `list` output.
-
-## Filtering Issues
-
-Use Sentry search syntax with the `-q` flag:
-
-```bash
-# Unresolved issues only
-sentry issue list -q "is:unresolved"
-
-# By error level
-sentry issue list -q "level:error"
-
-# By specific error type
-sentry issue list -q "TRPCError"
-
-# Assigned to someone
-sentry issue list -q "assigned:me"
-```
-
-## Sorting
-
-```bash
-sentry issue list -s date   # most recently seen (default)
-sentry issue list -s new    # newest issues first
-sentry issue list -s freq   # most frequent
-sentry issue list -s user   # most users affected
-```
-
-## Pagination
-
-Default limit is 25. Use `-n` and `-c` for more:
-
-```bash
-sentry issue list -n 50        # first 50
-sentry issue list -c next      # next page
-sentry issue list -c prev      # previous page
-```
-
-## JSON Output
-
-For programmatic use or detailed inspection:
-
-```bash
-# Full JSON
-sentry issue list --json
-
-# Select specific fields
-sentry issue list --json --fields shortId,title,count,lastSeen,priority
-
-# View issue as JSON
-sentry issue view <ID> --json
-```

-# Issue Commands Reference
-
-## `sentry issue list`

-
-```bash

-| ---- | ----- | ------- | ----------- |
-| `--query` | `-q` | — | Search query (Sentry search syntax) |
-| `--limit` | `-n` | `25` | Maximum number of issues |
-| `--sort` | `-s` | `date` | Sort by: `date`, `new`, `freq`, `user` |
-| `--period` | `-t` | `90d` | Time period (e.g. `24h`, `14d`, `90d`) |
-| `--cursor` | `-c` | — | Pagination (`next`, `prev`) |
-| `--compact` | — | auto | Single-line rows |
-| `--fresh` | `-f` | — | Bypass cache, fetch fresh data |
-| `--json` | — | — | Output as JSON |
-| `--fields` | — | — | Comma-separated fields for JSON output |
-
-### JSON Fields
-
-| Field | Type | Description |
-| ----- | ---- | ----------- |
-| `id` | string | Numeric issue ID |
-| `shortId` | string | Human-readable short ID (e.g. `PROJ-ABC`) |
-| `title` | string | Issue title |
-| `culprit` | string | Culprit string |
-| `count` | string | Total event count |
-| `userCount` | number | Number of affected users |
-| `firstSeen` | string | First occurrence (ISO 8601) |
-| `lastSeen` | string | Most recent occurrence (ISO 8601) |
-| `level` | string | Severity level |
-| `status` | string | Issue status |
-| `priority` | string | Triage priority |
-| `platform` | string | Platform |
-| `permalink` | string | URL to the issue in Sentry |
-| `project` | object | Project info |
-| `metadata` | object | Issue metadata |
-| `assignedTo` | unknown | Assigned user or team |
-| `substatus` | string | Issue substatus |
-| `isUnhandled` | boolean | Whether the issue is unhandled |
-| `seerFixabilityScore` | number | Seer AI fixability score (0-1) |
-

-
-```bash
